content:
# Matthew Chapter 16 — The PNEUMA Bible (Dynamic)
**Translator:** PNEUMA | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/MAT_016_page_001.jpg, MAT_016_page_002.jpg

### Demanding a Sign

1. The Pharisees and Sadducees came to test Jesus by demanding that he show them a sign from heaven.

2. He replied, "You know how to read the weather: in the evening you say, 'It'll be fine tomorrow — red sky tonight.'

3. And in the morning, 'Storm coming — the sky is red and overcast.' You can read the sky, but you can't read the signs of the times?

4. A wicked, faithless generation demands a sign, but the only sign it will get is the sign of Jonah." Then he turned and walked away.

### The Yeast of the Pharisees

5. When the disciples crossed to the other side, they realized they had forgotten to bring bread.

6. "Be on your guard," Jesus told them. "Watch out for the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees."

7. They discussed this among themselves: "He must be saying that because we forgot bread."

8. Jesus knew what they were thinking. "You have so little faith!" he said. "Why are you worried about having no bread?

9. Don't you get it yet? Don't you remember the five loaves for the five thousand — and how many baskets of leftovers you collected?

10. Or the seven loaves for the four thousand — and how many baskets then?

11. How can you not understand that I wasn't talking about bread? Watch out for the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees."

12. Then they finally understood — he wasn't warning them about bread yeast. He was warning them about the teaching of the Pharisees and Sadducees.

### Peter's Declaration

13. When Jesus arrived in the region of Caesarea Philippi, he asked his disciples, "Who do people say the Son of Man is?"

14. They replied, "Some say John the Baptist, others say Elijah, and still others say Jeremiah or one of the prophets."

15. "But what about you?" he asked. "Who do you say I am?"

16. Simon Peter spoke up: "You are the Messiah, the Son of the living God."

17. Jesus replied, "You are blessed, Simon son of Jonah, because no human being revealed this to you — my Father in heaven did.

18. And I tell you this: you are Peter — 'the Rock' — and on this rock I will build my community, and not even the gates of death will be able to overpower it.

19. I will give you the keys of the kingdom of heaven. Whatever you lock on earth will be locked in heaven, and whatever you unlock on earth will be unlocked in heaven."

20. Then he strictly warned his disciples not to tell anyone that he was the Messiah.

### Jesus Predicts His Death

21. From that point on, Jesus began to explain to his disciples that he had to go to Jerusalem, where he would suffer terribly at the hands of the elders, chief priests, and teachers of the law. He would be killed — and on the third day he would be raised to life.

22. Peter pulled him aside and started to scold him. "Absolutely not, Lord! This must never happen to you!"

23. Jesus turned on him and said, "Get away from me, Satan! You are a dangerous obstacle, because you're thinking from a human perspective, not God's."

24. Then Jesus said to his disciples, "If any of you want to follow me, you must give up your own way, take up your cross, and follow me.

25. If you try to hold on to your life, you'll lose it. But if you give up your life for my sake, you'll find it.

26. What good is it to gain the whole world but lose your own soul? What could you ever trade for your soul?

27. The Son of Man will come in his Father's glory with his angels, and then he will reward each person according to what they have done.

28. I tell you the truth: some of you standing here right now will not die before you see the Son of Man coming in his kingdom."

---
## PNEUMA's Meaning Notes

**Verse 16 — "the Messiah, the Son of the living God":** This is the hinge of the entire Gospel. Everything before this builds toward this confession; everything after flows from it. Peter doesn't just say "a prophet" or "a great teacher." He says Messiah — the anointed king Israel has been waiting for — and Son of God — sharing the very nature of the living God.

**Verse 18 — "Peter... rock... community":** Jesus makes a wordplay: "You are Petros (Rock), and on this petra (bedrock) I will build my ekklesia." The Aramaic behind both words is Kepha — one word, no distinction. What Jesus builds is an ἐκκλησία — not a building or institution, but a called-together assembly, a community united by this confession. And "the gates of death" (Hades) will not hold it — death itself cannot defeat what Jesus is building.

**Verse 23 — From "blessed" to "Satan":** The whiplash is intentional. Peter goes from the highest commendation in the Gospels (v.17) to the harshest rebuke (v.23) in six verses. The same person can be a channel of divine revelation and an instrument of human resistance to God's plan. The difference: thinking God's thoughts vs. thinking human thoughts.

**Verse 24 — "take up your cross":** The second time Jesus uses this image (first in 10:38). Now it's no longer theoretical — he has just told them he will be killed. "Take up your cross" means: follow me on the road to death. Discipleship is not improvement; it's execution of the old self.

— PNEUMA, AI Translator
